CREATE TABLE file_table (ft_name varchar, ft_cont long varbinary, primary key (ft_name));

CREATE PROCEDURE insert_files (in fname varchar) { declare strout_handle any; strout_handle := file_to_string_output(fname); insert into file_table (ft_name, ft_cont) values (fname, strout_handle); strout_handle := file_to_string_output(fname, 10); insert into file_table (concat(ft_name, '_1'), strout_handle) values (fname, strout_handle); strout_handle := file_to_string_output(fname, 10, 20); insert into file_table (concat(ft_name, '_2'), strout_handle) values (fname, strout_handle); };
CALL insert_files('file1.txt');
CALL insert_files('file2.txt');
CREATE TABLE permissions_table (file_name varchar, user_id int, can_access boolean, primary key(file_name, user_id));
INSERT INTO permissions_table (file_name, user_id, can_access) VALUES ('file1.txt', 1, true);
INSERT INTO permissions_table (file_name, user_id, can_access) VALUES ('file1.txt', 2, false);
SELECT ft_name FROM file_table WHERE ft_name IN (SELECT file_name FROM permissions_table WHERE user_id = 1 AND can_access = true);
SELECT ft_name, ft_cont FROM file_table WHERE ft_cont IS NOT NULL;
SELECT ft_name FROM file_table WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM permissions_table WHERE file_name = ft_name AND user_id = 1 AND can_access = true);
SELECT ft_name, ft_cont FROM file_table WHERE ft_cont LIKE '%' || (SELECT ft_name FROM file_table WHERE ft_name = 'file1.txt');
SELECT CONCAT(ft_name, ' - ', CAST(ft_cont AS varchar)) AS detailed_info FROM file_table;
CREATE TABLE additional_files (file_name varchar, owner_id int, primary key(file_name));
INSERT INTO additional_files (file_name, owner_id) VALUES ('file3.txt', 1);
INSERT INTO additional_files (file_name, owner_id) VALUES ('file4.txt', 2);
INSERT INTO additional_files (file_name, owner_id) VALUES ('file5.txt', 1);
SELECT a.file_name, f.ft_cont FROM additional_files a JOIN file_table f ON a.file_name = f.ft_name WHERE a.owner_id = 1;
CREATE TABLE log_table (log_id int primary key, file_name varchar, action varchar);
INSERT INTO log_table (log_id, file_name, action) VALUES (1, 'file1.txt', 'insert');
INSERT INTO log_table (log_id, file_name, action) VALUES (2, 'file2.txt', 'insert');
SELECT l.file_name, l.action FROM log_table l JOIN file_table f ON l.file_name = f.ft_name WHERE l.action = 'insert';
INSERT INTO file_table (ft_name, ft_cont) SELECT file_name, file_to_string_output(file_name) FROM additional_files WHERE owner_id = 1;
